page: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the FS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is limited to an 8 GB disk, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the F2s demonstrates entry-level performance characteristics. Synthetic CPU benchmarks, including stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, place the processor performance in the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks (137.06 GB/sec) and large uncached blocks (14.46 GB/sec read, 11.46 GB/sec write) also rank in the bottom 10%. Similarly, database operations and cryptographic tasks, such as the Passmark CPU encryption test, register weak to poor scores. For lightweight workloads, the server achieved 276.69 tokens per second for prompt processing and 65.75 tokens per second for text generation using a small 135M parameter LLM.

From a resource density perspective, the F2s offers a low-capacity compute profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. While it features dedicated CPU allocation rather than shared-core burstable resources, the older Intel Xeon Broadwell architecture and low memory capacity limit its utility for modern data-intensive applications. The primary tradeoff is the constraint of 4.0 GB of RAM and 8 GB of local storage, which restricts the server to lightweight utility tasks. This virtual machine is appropriate for basic web serving, small-scale Redis caching, simple text processing, or minor development and testing environments that do not require high memory or storage throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 virtual machine from the FS family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of memory and 8 GB of local storage. The instance is Premium Storage capable but lacks GPU resources. In benchmark testing, the F2s performs in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and memory bandwidth results placing in the bottom 10%. It handles lightweight tasks, such as small-model LLM inference (135M parameters) at 65.75 tokens per second for text generation, but struggles with resource-intensive operations. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this server is appropriate for low-demand workloads, including basic web serving, minor database operations, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 8 GB of local storage, designed for entry-level compute workloads and basic utility tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2s is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 8 GB of local storage.
tagline: An entry-level, compute-focused virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and low memory footprint for basic utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 vCPUs at 2.3 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 8 GB of local storage
Premium Storage capable for external disk attachment
Low-tier benchmark performance across CPU and memory tests
Appropriate for basic web serving, light database tasks, and testing
